_IMPORTANT_READ_BEFORE_EDITING: ALWAYS read docs/common-errors/traefik_skip_unhealthy_check.md, docs/common-errors/container_name_conflicts.md, and electron/docker-templates/README.md before adding or editing templates!
_healthcheck_policy: NEVER add wget/curl healthchecks to web apps (minimal images lack these tools → unhealthy containers → Traefik filters them out → 404 errors). ONLY add healthchecks to databases using native tools (pg_isready, mysqladmin, redis-cli). For multi-service stacks, use depends_on with condition: service_healthy to ensure databases start before web apps.
_password_generation: Generated passwords are URL-safe by default (alphanumeric only) because many templates embed secrets in connection strings (postgres://user:pass@host/db) and some upstream apps do not percent-encode correctly. Secrets that explicitly require hex are generated as hex based on the envHint description (e.g. "64 hex chars") and well-known key names (OPENSSL_KEY, ENCRYPTION_KEY). See electron/ipc/docker-stacks.ts:generateSecretForHint().
_project_name_uniqueness: CRITICAL: Project names must be GLOBALLY UNIQUE per server. Before deployment, ServerCompass checks for existing Docker containers with pattern '{projectName}-*'. If found, deployment is blocked to prevent volume reuse issues. MySQL/PostgreSQL only initialize passwords on FIRST startup - reusing old volumes causes 'Access denied' errors because new passwords in .env are ignored. Solution: Use unique project names (e.g., 'ghost-blog' instead of 'ghost') or completely remove old containers first with 'docker compose down -v'. See docs/common-errors/container_name_conflicts.md for details.
